evidence_quotes.quote: A trend that has become increasingly common in the game game is games that must always be connected to servers even when it is not relevant, for example, singleplayer games.
This lifespan of these games that you buy is then somewhat odd and may result in a game you bought becoming completely harmless just a few years after you have bought it.
I think gaming manufacturers should be able to be held accountable for their products to be gamble in some form even if the judgement chooses to close down the servers of the games you have bought.

paragraphs.text: Hejsan, I strongly agree that more protection for consumers is needed in today’s digital landscape, an area I think there is more to talk about is “TV games”. A trend that has become increasingly common in the game game is games that must always be connected to servers even when it is not relevant, for example, singleplayer games. Why I think this is relevant is because these games are more or less best placed before the date when servers are required from the manufacturer. This lifespan of these games that you buy is then somewhat odd and may result in a game you bought becoming completely harmless just a few years after you have bought it. This is then all the more relevant in the case of minors where the judgment does not directly have an income. I myself struggled to afford games during my childhood, but at that time I knew at least that the games I bought could play for years ahead as long as I did not lose my disc, but now in this digital landscape this is no longer given. I think gaming manufacturers should be able to be held accountable for their products to be gamble in some form even if the judgement chooses to close down the servers of the games you have bought. In order to collaborate, I therefore ask whether you could expand the scope you talk about the digital product ‘TV games’ and what is required by the manufacturer when deciding to sell these products in the EU. Thank you for taking time to read this and I wish you every success in the future.
